finding: Slovak firms' bank loan availability expectations deteriorated moderately in wave 38
bullets: A net 5% of Slovak firms (n=130) expected bank loan availability to deteriorate in wave 38, moderating 3.2 pp from a near-neutral net 2% in the previous wave — converging with the EA, where expectations also worsened by 3.4 pp to a net 4%, signalling tighter financing conditions ahead.

Slovak firms were considerably more pessimistic about trade credit availability than EA peers, with a net 10% expecting deterioration (n=79) compared with a net 1% for the EA — a gap of 9.4 pp, suggesting persistent local supply tightness not yet visible in EA-wide data.

Expectations for credit line and bank overdraft availability edged further negative for Slovak firms (net 7%, n=138, easing marginally by 1.3 pp), remaining 3.2 pp more pessimistic than the EA's net 4% — indicating continued constraints on short-term liquidity options.
chart_subtitle: A net 5% of Slovak firms expected bank loan availability to deteriorate.
